name: gh-review-tool
description: Go CLI tool that fetches a GitHub PR diff, runs configurable review rules, and posts line-level comments as a single PR review

milestones.name: scaffold
milestones.description: Go module init, cobra CLI skeleton, flag parsing for --repo, --pr, --token

milestones.name: github-client
milestones.description: GitHub API client with token auth, PR metadata and diff fetching

milestones.name: diff-engine
milestones.description: Unified diff parser and line classification engine

milestones.name: review-engine
milestones.description: Rule interface, built-in rules, and RuleSet engine

milestones.name: pr-poster
milestones.description: Convert findings to GitHub comments and submit PR review

milestones.name: polish
milestones.description: Config file, output formatters, exit codes, dry-run, error handling

features.description: Initialize Go module, vendor cobra, implement root + review subcommand with --repo, --pr-number, --github-token flags and help text
expectedBehavior: `go build` compiles without errors
`gh-review-tool --help` prints usage with flags
`gh-review-tool review --help` prints subcommand help
Missing required --pr flag produces a clear error message

features.description: Implement GitHub client struct wrapping go-github with token auth, configurable timeout (default 30s), rate-limit awareness, and wrapped errors
expectedBehavior: Client initializes from GITHUB_TOKEN env var or --github-token flag
Authenticated requests to GitHub API succeed
Non-200 responses return wrapped errors with status code and message
Rate-limit responses wait and retry

features.description: Implement FetchPR and FetchDiff methods on GitHub client returning PR metadata (author, title, refs) and raw unified diff body
expectedBehavior: FetchPR returns PR number, title, author login, base SHA, head SHA
FetchDiff returns raw diff string matching `git diff` format
Both methods accept owner, repo, and PR number arguments
Both methods return descriptive errors for 404, 403, and network failures

features.description: Implement DiffParser that converts a raw unified diff string into []DiffFile, each containing []Hunk with []Line (type: added/removed/context, old/new line numbers, content)
expectedBehavior: Parsed DiffFile slice has one entry per file in the diff
Each Hunk has correct oldStart, oldLines, newStart, newLines
Each Line is correctly classified as added, removed, or context
Line numbers are correct for both old and new positions
Binary files are detected and stored with isBinary=true

features.description: Implement LineClassifier utility that scans parsed lines for common issues: leftover debug statements (fmt.Println, log.Printf), TODO/FIXME comments, missing error checks on Go returns, and large function bodies (>50 lines added in one hunk)
expectedBehavior: Debug statement detector flags fmt.Println and log.Printf in added lines
TODO detector flags TODO and FIXME comments in added lines
Error check detector flags added lines calling funcs returning error without assigning to _ or err
Large hunk detector flags hunks with >50 added lines
Each detection returns a Finding with file, line, severity, and message

features.description: Define Rule interface (Name, Severity, Check([]DiffFile) []Finding) and implement 3 built-in rules: NoDebugStatements, NoTODOsInCode, LargeDiffWarning using the line classifier
expectedBehavior: Rule interface can be implemented by external packages
NoDebugStatements rule produces findings for each flagged debug line
NoTODOsInCode rule produces findings for each flagged TODO comment
LargeDiffWarning rule produces one finding per overlarge hunk
Each finding includes file path, line number, severity (warning/error), and human-readable message

features.description: Implement RuleSet engine that accepts a slice of Rules, runs Check on each against the parsed diff, deduplicates and sorts findings by severity then file then line
expectedBehavior: All registered rules execute against the full parsed diff
Findings are deduplicated by (file, line, rule name)
Findings are sorted: errors first, then warnings, then info
Empty rules slice produces zero findings
RuleSet is reusable across multiple diffs

features.description: Implement Poster that converts []Finding to []github.DraftReviewComment with correct path, line, side (LEFT/RIGHT), body, and submits a single PR Review via GitHub Checks API (event=COMMENT)
expectedBehavior: Each finding maps to a DraftReviewComment with correct file path and line number
Side is LEFT for removed lines, RIGHT for added lines
Body truncates at 65536 chars (GitHub limit) with truncation notice
SubmitReview posts all comments as a single review with event=COMMENT
Zero findings skips the API call entirely
API errors return wrapped errors with the failed comment details

features.description: Wire full pipeline in the review CLI command: fetch diff -> parse -> classify -> run rules -> post results. Add --dry-run flag that runs pipeline but skips posting, printing findings to stdout instead
expectedBehavior: `gh-review-tool review --repo owner/repo --pr 42` runs full pipeline and posts review
`--dry-run` prints all findings as formatted table to stdout without API call
Exit code is 0 when no findings, 1 when findings exist, 2 on errors
Pipeline aborts early with clear message on fetch or parse failure
Progress messages print to stderr, results to stdout

features.description: Implement YAML config file loading from .gh-review-tool.yaml (search cwd and parents). Config supports: enabled_rules list, severity overrides per rule, ignore_paths globs, max-hunk-size threshold, and custom rule options
expectedBehavior: Missing config file is not an error (defaults apply)
enabled_rules: ['no-debug'] only runs matching rules
ignore_paths: ['vendor/*', '*.pb.go'] skips matching files
severity overrides: 'no-debug: error' upgrades finding severity
CLI --config flag overrides default config file path
Config values merge with CLI flags (CLI takes precedence)

features.description: Implement --format flag with table (default), json, and summary modes. Table shows per-file grouping with line numbers. JSON outputs all findings as structured JSON array. Summary shows counts per severity. Add --verbose flag for structured logging
expectedBehavior: --format table prints findings grouped by file with severity, line, message columns
--format json prints a valid JSON array with all finding fields
--format summary prints '3 errors, 5 warnings, 2 info' with breakdown per rule
--verbose prints info-level logs to stderr (API calls, parsing stats, timing)
JSON output is parseable by jq
